title: VCITE Citation Object
description: Verified Citation with Inline Text Evidence (VCITE) v1.0. A passage-level, cryptographically verifiable citation object per the VCITE specification §4.

vcite.description: VCITE specification version. MUST be "1.0" for objects conforming to this schema.

id.description: Unique identifier for this citation object. Format: "vcite-" followed by 8 lowercase hex characters.

captured_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when this citation was captured.

captured_by.description: Whether this citation was created by a human author or an AI model.

Source.description: Metadata about the source document being cited (§4.2).

authors.description: Authors of the source document. Format: "Last, First" per bibliographic convention.

year.description: Publication year of the source document.

url.description: URL where the source document can be accessed.

archive_url.description: Archived URL (e.g., Perma.cc, Internet Archive) providing a persistent copy.

source_type.description: Classification of the source document type.

Target.description: Passage location and fingerprint within the source document (§4.3). Extends W3C Web Annotation TextQuoteSelector.

text_before.description: Up to 50 Unicode code points of context immediately before the cited passage.

text_exact.description: The verbatim cited passage. Whitespace is preserved as-is; normalization occurs only during hash computation.

text_after.description: Up to 50 Unicode code points of context immediately after the cited passage.

hash.description: SHA-256 fingerprint of the passage computed per §5. Format: "sha256:" followed by 64 lowercase hex characters.

char_start.description: Zero-based Unicode code point offset of the passage start within the source document.

char_end.description: Zero-based Unicode code point offset of the passage end (exclusive) within the source document.

section.description: Section of the source document containing the passage.

fragment_url.description: URL with fragment identifier pointing to the passage (e.g., text fragment URL).

Relation.description: Semantic relationship between the citing document and the cited passage (§4.4). Core vocabulary of 7 values plus extension mechanism via "x-" prefix.

Enrichment.description: Optional third-party enrichment data (§4.5). Properties are extensible; common enrichments listed here.

retraction_watch.description: Whether the source appears in the Retraction Watch database.

verified_at.description: When the enrichment data was last verified.

ConformanceL2.description: Level 2 conformance: L1 plus context windows and at least one resolvable URL.

ConformanceL3.description: Level 3 conformance: L2 plus archive URL and fragment URL.
